title: boechera yorkii
intro: Boechera yorkii, also known as White Buffalo Cloverbloom, is a rare and endangered perennial plant species native to the White Mountains of New Hampshire. It belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is often referred to as a wild mustard or a wild cabbage. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can be used to treat various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Boechera yorkii can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, providing potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
